lens_disposition.reason: The throughput signal is real: add-liquidity-multi accounts for 29.86% of run runtime, 35.68% read_count, 37.92% read_length, 45.44% write_count, and 44.09% write_length, with write_count the promoted near-binding axis. Code and traces show this comes from the contract's 150-iteration fold over nested dlmm-core add-liquidity calls and their consensus-visible map/var writes. The actionable node-side storage handle below reduces wall time in SQLite side-store writes, but it does not reduce deterministic Clarity write_count/read_length/write_length without a contract change or HIP-class cost/VM behavior change.

targets.evidence: Representative traces 37ad67a3, 408f81be, 92639041, and 9f0416be all run under Transaction -> try_mine_tx_with_len -> PersistentWritableMarfStore::put_all_data after VM execution. That span loops over 1401-1502 staged edits and spends 515-770 ms almost entirely in SqliteConnection::put. The code path is RollbackWrapper::commit collecting bottom-level edits, calling ClarityBackingStore::put_all_data, then PersistentWritableMarfStore::put_all_data converting each value to a MARFValue and invoking SqliteConnection::put once per item before MARF::insert_batch. SqliteConnection::put is a single REPLACE INTO data_table statement via conn.execute, so this target is storage mechanics, not Clarity cost accounting. The fifth representative, 74a98eed, was inspected and is instead dominated by MARF Trie::walk_backptr inside VM reads; it does not invalidate the put target but should not be used as the primary replay sample for it.
targets.proposed_change: Add a bulk side-store write helper beside SqliteConnection::put, for example SqliteConnection::put_many(conn, items), that prepares the REPLACE INTO data_table (key, value) VALUES (?, ?) statement once against the existing rusqlite Transaction and executes it for all converted side-store values. Use that helper in PersistentWritableMarfStore::put_all_data after building the MARF keys/values, and in MemoryBackingStore::put_all_data for parity. Keep MARF::insert_batch unchanged and preserve the exact key/value strings and error mapping.
targets.verification_plan: Check Clarity backing-store unit coverage for put/get round trips, rollback commit behavior, and MARF side-store reads by hash. Add focused regression coverage if no test asserts that batched put_all_data writes exactly the same data_table rows as individual puts. Then run the targeted replay below and compare put and put_all_data spans, plus transaction duration.

evidence_queries.purpose: Rank the storage write span in the full baseline run.
evidence_queries.key_observation: SqliteConnection::put is span_id 60 with 297586.64 ms self wall across 1038880 calls; avg self is 286.449 us/call.

evidence_queries.purpose: Confirm add-liquidity-multi is the promoted throughput family and identify the near-binding axis.
evidence_queries.key_observation: add-liquidity-multi has 592 calls and consumes 45.44% of run write_count, 44.09% write_length, 37.92% read_length, 35.68% read_count, and 29.86% runtime.

evidence_queries.purpose: List concrete add-liquidity-multi transactions and their Clarity write volume.
evidence_queries.key_observation: The top five representatives run for 1937-2243 ms and carry 1399-1650 Clarity write_count each.

evidence_queries.purpose: Inspect representative 37ad67a3 and isolate post-VM side-store writes.
evidence_queries.key_observation: put_all_data is 565.408 ms; child SqliteConnection::put is 539.374 ms self across 1502 calls.

evidence_queries.purpose: Inspect representative 408f81be and isolate post-VM side-store writes.
evidence_queries.key_observation: put_all_data is 540.743 ms; child SqliteConnection::put is 515.217 ms self across 1502 calls.

evidence_queries.purpose: Inspect representative 92639041 and isolate post-VM side-store writes.
evidence_queries.key_observation: put_all_data is 791.306 ms; child SqliteConnection::put is 769.613 ms self across 1502 calls.

evidence_queries.purpose: Inspect representative 9f0416be and isolate post-VM side-store writes.
evidence_queries.key_observation: put_all_data is 541.240 ms; child SqliteConnection::put is 517.346 ms self across 1401 calls.

evidence_queries.purpose: Characterize per-call shape for the put span.
evidence_queries.key_observation: SqliteConnection::put has p95 self 417.44 us and p99 self 569.0 us across 1038880 calls.

evidence_queries.purpose: Confirm put can dominate individual replay blocks.
evidence_queries.key_observation: SqliteConnection::put has max per-block self wall 978.535 ms and p99 block self wall 491.435 ms.

verification_replay.rationale: Replay the representatives where post-VM side-store writes dominate, then compare transaction latency and the put/put_all_data spans.

invocations.label: write-heavy txs
invocations.purpose: Measure whether batching side-store writes reduces the SQLite put-heavy add-liquidity executions.

global_materiality_note: The lens itself is contract-authored Clarity write/read budget and is not node-actionable for tenure throughput. The SQLite side-store batching target is still broadly relevant to write-heavy Clarity transactions beyond this DLMM family, but it should be prioritized as a block-processing latency optimization, not a throughput-budget fix.
